title: buxaceae
intro: The Buxaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, specifically a group of evergreen trees and shrubs that are native to the Northern Hemisphere. This family is characterized by its small, four-petaled flowers and distinctive, often leathery leaves. The plants within the Buxaceae family are often found growing in woodland environments and are valued for their ornamental and medicinal properties. Examples of plants belonging to this family include the box tree (Buxus sempervirens) and the pachysandra (Pachysandra terminalis).
